audienceResearch: Public-page evidence from betabots.rachkovan.com plus project context: open-source synthetic beta users, local dashboard, installable skills, cloud review request, Betabook, Destiny, and evidence artifacts.
researchSources: public: https://betabots.rachkovan.com/ live landing page on 2026-07-04
project: Betabots open-source repository and dashboard intent
assumption: primary visitors are founders, product/design leads, open-source contributors, and agency/consulting buyers

audienceSegments.name: Founder or product owner
audienceSegments.evidence: Landing positions Betabots as a way to get product evidence before real users are available.

audienceSegments.name: Designer or UX researcher
audienceSegments.evidence: Landing emphasizes screenshots, action timelines, truthful synthetic users, and evidence packs.

audienceSegments.name: Developer contributor
audienceSegments.evidence: Landing and repo include open-source install paths for Codex, Claude Code, Cursor, docs, GitHub, and AGPL.

audienceSegments.name: Mobile founder doing a quick check
audienceSegments.evidence: Landing has mobile layout and cloud request path; mobile visitors need a fast proof path.

roles.role: seed-stage founder evaluating whether Betabots can replace early beta feedback
roles.past: I have no patience for fake AI praise because product mistakes cost runway.
roles.discovery: A founder friend sent the Betabots link after I complained about slow beta recruitment.
roles.goal: See real evidence, understand what a run produces, and decide whether to request a review.
roles.lifeGoal: Protect my company, runway, and reputation by choosing feedback systems that reveal truth early.

roles.role: UX researcher skeptical of synthetic users
roles.past: I value real user research and only trust synthetic methods when artifacts are inspectable and limitations are explicit.
roles.discovery: I opened the site after seeing a claim about truthful synthetic beta users.
roles.goal: Determine whether the method, dashboard, and evidence trail are credible.
roles.lifeGoal: Keep research decisions honest and avoid selling false confidence to teams.

roles.role: open-source developer deciding whether to install the skill
roles.past: I try agent plugins carefully because bad tools clutter my local setup.
roles.discovery: I found Betabots through the repository and clicked to the landing.
roles.goal: Find the install command, supported harnesses, docs, and license clarity.
roles.lifeGoal: Use tools I can inspect and extend without becoming dependent on opaque SaaS.

roles.role: mobile founder checking the offer between meetings
roles.past: I am interested but impatient; if the site does not show proof fast, I will leave.
roles.discovery: I opened the link from a chat on my phone.
roles.goal: Quickly view a beta/demo and decide whether to request a free sample.
roles.lifeGoal: Spend my attention only on tools that can quickly improve product decisions.

ideaRules.idea: Make the demo an actual dashboard backed by real Betabot artifacts, not a static marketing page.
Keep install commands as one real command per harness.
Show free sample expectations and paid full analytic clearly near the form.
